title: Cold email is dead. Here's what replaced it.

slides.headline: Cold email is dead
slides.visual_note: Email icon with X through it, red background

slides.headline: The data
slides.body: Average cold email open rate: 17%. Reply rate: 1-2%. That's 98% of your effort wasted.

slides.headline: Why it died
slides.body: AI spam tools flooded inboxes. Gmail/Outlook got smarter. Everyone sends the same templates. Buyers are numb.

slides.headline: What replaced it: Signal-based outreach
slides.body: Stop blasting lists. Start monitoring buying signals. Job changes. Funding rounds. Tech stack changes. Product launches.

slides.headline: The new stack
slides.body: Clay for signal monitoring. LinkedIn Sales Nav for filtering. Loom for video replies. No mass email tools.
slides.visual_note: Tool logos in a flow diagram

slides.headline: The pattern that works
slides.body: 1. They post about a problem. 2. You reply with genuine insight. 3. They check your profile. 4. They DM you.

slides.headline: Real numbers
slides.body: Cold email: 2% reply rate. Signal-based DM: 34% reply rate. Same effort. 17x better results.
slides.visual_note: Comparison bar chart, green vs red

slides.headline: Where to find signals
slides.body: LinkedIn: job changes, promotions, posts about struggles. Twitter/X: complaints, tool recommendations. ProductHunt: launch days.

slides.headline: The shift
slides.body: Cold email = spray and pray. Signal outreach = sniper approach. Less volume. Higher quality. Better close rate.
slides.visual_note: Spray can vs sniper scope icons

cta_slide.headline: I break down my outreach systems weekly
